Interface Mutable

All Superinterfaces:
Serializable
All Known Implementing Classes:
File, Fileset, Invocation, Result
public interface Mutable extends Serializable
A reference to one or more resources that might have been modified since the reference was created

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final Mutable
    CHANGED
    A Mutable instance that always returns true from modifiedSince(Serializable)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Serializable
    currently(Object obj)
    Returns an object's current state
    Serializable
    currentState()
    Returns a serializable representation of the current state of the resources
    default boolean
    modifiedSince(Serializable oldState)
    Compares current and previous state
    static Serializable
    snapshots(Stream<?> values)
    Returns a representation of the current state of a stream of resources

  • Field Details

    • CHANGED

      static final Mutable CHANGED
      A Mutable instance that always returns true from modifiedSince(Serializable)

  • Method Details

    • currentState

      Serializable currentState()
      Returns a serializable representation of the current state of the resources
      Returns:
      an object

    • modifiedSince

      default boolean modifiedSince(Serializable oldState)
      Compares current and previous state
      Parameters:
      oldState - a previous state returned by currentState()
      Returns:
      true if the current state is different to the old one

    • currently

      static Serializable currently(Object obj)
      Returns an object's current state
      Parameters:
      obj - the object
      Returns:
      the object's current state if it is mutable, otherwise null

    • snapshots

      static Serializable snapshots(Stream<?> values)
      Returns a representation of the current state of a stream of resources
      Parameters:
      values - the stream of objects
      Returns:
      a map of objects to their current state
